resetInfoToast: Form reset to defaults (not saved yet)
loadErrorSubtitle: Could not load settings. You may need the console:settings permission.
intro: System name is stored on the server and exposed to everyone via public API (no login). Other fields require an authenticated admin with console:settings.
apiBasePlaceholder: Optional note (not used for API calls)
apiBaseHint: Free-text note stored with system settings. The app continues to use the build-time API URL for requests.

health.subtitle: Check whether the API, database, object storage, background jobs, job worker and scheduler processes, Langfuse tracing host (when configured on the server), and registered data sources are reachable.
health.processesEmpty: No worker or scheduler heartbeats received yet.
health.dataSourcesEmpty: No data sources to check, or you need console:data_sources to see connection tests here.

overview.subtitle: Security, access, database connections, local users, feature toggles, and console settings—everything available from the console sidebar.
overview.nudge: Your permission catalog only defines <codeTag>all</codeTag>. <setupLink>Set up operation keys and roles</setupLink> before delegating access.
overview.introLead: Each card links to a console page when your role allows it; paths are under <codePath>/console/…</codePath>.

health.title: System health
health.description: Verify API, database, storage, job queue, and data source connectivity in one place.

permissions.title: Permissions & roles
permissions.description: Define operation keys in the catalog, assign them to roles, and map IdP realm roles (OIDC) or local users to those roles.

dataSecurity.title: Data security
dataSecurity.description: Review sharing issues (missing owner, Manage on Others), manage access groups, and fix ACL from the console.

dataSources.title: Data sources
dataSources.description: Register databases and other connections (PostgreSQL, Neo4j, etc.) used by datasets and pipelines.

usersToggles.title: Users & feature toggles
usersToggles.description: Manage local users (when not using a central IdP) and turn product areas such as articles, knowledge bases, or ontology UI on or off.

systemSettings.title: System settings
systemSettings.description: Reserved for deployment-specific options; extend here as new console settings are added.

featureToggles.subtitle: Enable or disable optional areas for everyone. Disabled areas are hidden from the sidebar and navigation.

evaluations.name: Evaluation
evaluations.description: KB QA evaluations (experimental). Query and expected-answer pairs to gauge RAG quality.

connectors.name: Connectors
connectors.description: External integrations with encrypted credentials and dataset targets for sync jobs.

agents.name: Agents
agents.description: Project workspaces with Deep Agents chat, files, git, and research tools.

media.name: Media library
media.description: Image and video collections with metadata, upload, and AI generation (experimental).

users.subtitleManaged: Manage local accounts and the Admin role (console access).
users.subtitleOidc: Users who have signed in to openKMS. Roles are managed in your identity provider.
users.deleteConfirm: Delete user "{{username}}"? This cannot be undone.
users.searchPlaceholderOidc: Search by username, email, or name…
users.emptyOidc: No users have signed in yet.
users.modalSubtitle: Creates a local account with a password.

accessGroups.subtitle: Groups bundle people for sharing. Add members here, then grant the group access on each channel, wiki space, or other item from that item's Sharing settings.
accessGroups.noGroups: No groups yet. Create one to get started.
accessGroups.emptyDetail: Select a group to edit members or view where it has been granted access.
accessGroups.membersHintLocal: Search to add users; changes save immediately.
accessGroups.membersHintOidc: Add each person by subject id (username or email may work). Changes save immediately.
accessGroups.noMembers: No members in this group yet.
accessGroups.shareHint: Sharing is configured per item. Open a channel, wiki space, or knowledge base and use its Sharing panel to grant this group access.
accessGroups.sharingHint: Items that already grant this group read, write, or manage access.
accessGroups.noSharedResources: Nothing is shared with this group yet.
accessGroups.name: Name
accessGroups.createModalSub: You can add members after the group is created.
accessGroups.deleteConfirm: Delete group "{{name}}"? Members are removed; sharing grants that reference this group may break until you update them.

dataSecurityIssues.subtitle: Sharing misconfigurations that weaken accountability or expose data more broadly than intended.
dataSecurityIssues.legacyCount: {{count}} leftover row(s) — no longer enforced; safe to ignore or delete from the database.
dataSecurityIssues.reviewRecommendedHint: These may be intentional. Confirm whether org-wide read matches your intent.

others_manage.heading: Manage granted to Others
others_manage.hint: Everyone signed in can change sharing on these resources.

others_write.heading: Write granted to Others
others_write.hint: Everyone signed in can edit content on these resources.

unknown_group.heading: Missing access group
unknown_group.hint: A group grant references a group that no longer exists.

empty_group.heading: Empty access group
empty_group.hint: A group grant references a group with no members — no one receives that access.

unknown_owner.heading: Owner account not found
unknown_owner.hint: The owner grant points to a user that no longer exists (local auth).

missing_owner.heading: Missing owner
missing_owner.hint: Sharing is configured but no owner is assigned in the ACL.

owner_no_permissions.heading: Owner has no permissions
owner_no_permissions.hint: The owner grant exists but grants no read, write, or manage access.

owner_no_manage.heading: Owner cannot manage sharing
owner_no_manage.hint: The owner cannot update sharing without an admin or another grantee with Manage.

implicit_others.heading: Others inherited from parent
implicit_others.hint: This resource has its own sharing rules but no explicit Others row; signed-in users may inherit access from a parent container.

others_read.heading: Read granted to Others
others_read.hint: Group-based sharing is configured, but everyone signed in can also read. Confirm this is intentional.

dataSources.toastRequiredFields: Name, host, and username are required
dataSources.deleteConfirm: Delete this data source? All linked datasets will be removed.
dataSources.subtitle: Manage PostgreSQL and Neo4j connections. Credentials are encrypted at rest.
dataSources.empty: No data sources yet. Create one to get started.
dataSources.deleteAllNeo4jTitle: Delete all nodes and relationships in Neo4j
dataSources.neo4jDeleteAllBody: Delete all nodes and relationships in this Neo4j database? This cannot be undone.

connectors.toastSecretsRequired: Enter the required API token (or other secrets) for this connector kind
connectors.toastDuplicateKey: Each key must be unique (in extra settings or secrets).
connectors.toastOutputsRequired: Choose a dataset for every output target.
connectors.toastOutputsIncomplete: Configure every output dataset, or clear all to save without datasets.
connectors.toastScheduleOutputsRequired: Turn off scheduled sync on the Schedule tab, or configure all output datasets.
connectors.toastScheduleAutoDisabled: Scheduled sync was turned off because no output datasets are configured.
connectors.syncRequiresOutputs: Configure output datasets before running sync.
connectors.cronRequiresOutputs: Configure all output datasets on the Output datasets tab before enabling scheduled sync.
connectors.toastDatasetsLoadFailed: Could not load datasets for the pickers. You may need ontology or dataset console access.
connectors.deleteConfirm: Delete this connector? Stored credentials will be removed.
connectors.subtitle: Sync connectors write to datasets; search_tool connectors supply on-demand web search for Agents.
connectors.empty: No connectors yet. Add one to store credentials for a supported integration.
connectors.secretLeaveBlank: leave blank to keep current value
connectors.outputSchemaHint: Shape of each search response (kind metadata). Instance outputs stay empty for search_tool connectors.
connectors.testSearchHint: Run a query through this connector (same path Agents use for the web_search tool).
connectors.probeHint: Send a live Tushare daily request (历史日线). Results are not written to datasets. Provide at least one of ts_code, trade_date, start_date, or end_date.
connectors.probeTradeDateDesc: Single trading date (all stocks on that day unless ts_code is set).
connectors.probeStartDateDesc: Range start date (use with end_date and/or ts_code).
connectors.playgroundHint: Adjust request parameters and send a live search. Overrides apply to this request only; saved connector settings are unchanged.
connectors.playgroundResponseEmpty: Send a request to see the response.
connectors.inputsHint: Declared by the connector type (for example the service base URL).
connectors.outputsHint: Pick an existing dataset for each target, or create a compatible table when the connector type defines a schema.
connectors.outputsOptionalHint: Output datasets are optional. Configure all targets before scheduled sync or manual sync; partial configuration is not allowed.
connectors.outputsClearScheduleHint: Scheduled sync is on. Clear every output target to save without datasets — scheduled sync will be turned off automatically on save.
connectors.outputsNotApplicable: This connector kind does not use output datasets.
connectors.provisionHint: Creates the PostgreSQL schema/table and registers a dataset row matching this connector slot.
connectors.provisionNoDataSources: No PostgreSQL data sources. Add one under Ontology → Data sources first.
connectors.syncDialogHint: Choose the date range to sync (start and end dates are inclusive).
connectors.syncDateOrderError: Start date must be on or before end date.
connectors.cronHint: Run the sync job automatically on a daily schedule. Times use the selected timezone.
connectors.settingsKvHint: Keys must be unique. Values can be text, numbers, true/false, or JSON objects/arrays.
connectors.searchToolSettingsHint: Set web_search_url to the full Zhipu web search endpoint (default https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/web_search). API base URL is configured under Inputs.
connectors.secretsKvHintCreate: Add one row per secret. Values are stored encrypted.
connectors.secretsKvHintEdit: Leave a value blank to keep the current stored secret. Enter a new value to replace it.
connectors.secretsExpectedKeys: This connector type expects these keys: {{keys}}.

datasets.toastRequiredFields: Data source, schema, and table are required
datasets.subtitle: Map PostgreSQL tables from data sources. They can be linked to object types and link types later.
datasets.emptyNeedPg: Add a PostgreSQL data source first.
datasets.emptyNone: No datasets yet. Create one to get started.

datasetSettings.generalHint: Display name is shown in lists and the dataset header. Schema and table cannot be changed here.

ontologySettings.objectTypeGeneralHint: Schema changes are made from the Object types list. Use Sharing to control who can view or edit instances.
ontologySettings.linkTypeGeneralHint: Schema changes are made from the Link types list. Use Sharing to control who can view or edit links.
